2156	 * Send functions always take a single argument of the type and return
2157	 * bytea.
2158	 */
2159	argList[0] = typeOid;
2160
2161	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2162	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2163		ereport(ERROR,
2164				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2165				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2166						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2167
2168	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != BYTEAOID)
2169		ereport(ERROR,
2170				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2171				 errmsg("type send function %s must return type %s",
2172						NameListToString(procname), "bytea")));
2173
2174	/* Just a warning for now, per comments in findTypeInputFunction */
2175	if (func_volatile(procOid) == PROVOLATILE_VOLATILE)
2176		ereport(WARNING,
2177				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2178				 errmsg("type send function %s should not be volatile",
2179						NameListToString(procname))));
2180
2181	return procOid;
2182}
2183
2184static Oid
2185findTypeTypmodinFunction(List *procname)
2186{
2187	Oid			argList[1];
2188	Oid			procOid;
2189
2190	/*
2191	 * typmodin functions always take one cstring[] argument and return int4.
2192	 */
2193	argList[0] = CSTRINGARRAYOID;
2194
2195	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2196	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2197		ereport(ERROR,
2198				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2199				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2200						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2201
2202	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != INT4OID)
2203		ereport(ERROR,
2204				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2205				 errmsg("typmod_in function %s must return type %s",
2206						NameListToString(procname), "integer")));
2207
2208	/* Just a warning for now, per comments in findTypeInputFunction */
2209	if (func_volatile(procOid) == PROVOLATILE_VOLATILE)
2210		ereport(WARNING,
2211				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2212				 errmsg("type modifier input function %s should not be volatile",
2213						NameListToString(procname))));
2214
2215	return procOid;
2216}
2217
2218static Oid
2219findTypeTypmodoutFunction(List *procname)
2220{
2221	Oid			argList[1];
2222	Oid			procOid;
2223
2224	/*
2225	 * typmodout functions always take one int4 argument and return cstring.
2226	 */
2227	argList[0] = INT4OID;
2228
2229	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2230	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2231		ereport(ERROR,
2232				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2233				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2234						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2235
2236	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != CSTRINGOID)
2237		ereport(ERROR,
2238				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2239				 errmsg("typmod_out function %s must return type %s",
2240						NameListToString(procname), "cstring")));
2241
2242	/* Just a warning for now, per comments in findTypeInputFunction */
2243	if (func_volatile(procOid) == PROVOLATILE_VOLATILE)
2244		ereport(WARNING,
2245				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2246				 errmsg("type modifier output function %s should not be volatile",
2247						NameListToString(procname))));
2248
2249	return procOid;
2250}
2251
2252static Oid
2253findTypeAnalyzeFunction(List *procname, Oid typeOid)
2254{
2255	Oid			argList[1];
2256	Oid			procOid;
2257
2258	/*
2259	 * Analyze functions always take one INTERNAL argument and return bool.
2260	 */
2261	argList[0] = INTERNALOID;
2262
2263	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2264	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2265		ereport(ERROR,
2266				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2267				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2268						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2269
2270	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != BOOLOID)
2271		ereport(ERROR,
2272				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2273				 errmsg("type analyze function %s must return type %s",
2274						NameListToString(procname), "boolean")));
2275
2276	return procOid;
2277}
2278
2279static Oid
2280findTypeSubscriptingFunction(List *procname, Oid typeOid)
2281{
2282	Oid			argList[1];
2283	Oid			procOid;
2284
2285	/*
2286	 * Subscripting support functions always take one INTERNAL argument and
2287	 * return INTERNAL.  (The argument is not used, but we must have it to
2288	 * maintain type safety.)
2289	 */
2290	argList[0] = INTERNALOID;
2291
2292	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2293	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2294		ereport(ERROR,
2295				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2296				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2297						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2298
2299	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != INTERNALOID)
2300		ereport(ERROR,
2301				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2302				 errmsg("type subscripting function %s must return type %s",
2303						NameListToString(procname), "internal")));
2304
2305	/*
2306	 * We disallow array_subscript_handler() from being selected explicitly,
2307	 * since that must only be applied to autogenerated array types.
2308	 */
2309	if (procOid == F_ARRAY_SUBSCRIPT_HANDLER)
2310		ereport(ERROR,
2311				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2312				 errmsg("user-defined types cannot use subscripting function %s",
2313						NameListToString(procname))));
2314
2315	return procOid;
2316}
2317
2318/*
2319 * Find suitable support functions and opclasses for a range type.
2320 */
2321
2322/*
2323 * Find named btree opclass for subtype, or default btree opclass if
2324 * opcname is NIL.
2325 */
2326static Oid
2327findRangeSubOpclass(List *opcname, Oid subtype)
2328{
2329	Oid			opcid;
2330	Oid			opInputType;
2331
2332	if (opcname != NIL)
2333	{
2334		opcid = get_opclass_oid(BTREE_AM_OID, opcname, false);
2335
2336		/*
2337		 * Verify that the operator class accepts this datatype. Note we will
2338		 * accept binary compatibility.
2339		 */
2340		opInputType = get_opclass_input_type(opcid);
2341		if (!IsBinaryCoercible(subtype, opInputType))
2342			ereport(ERROR,
2343					(errcode(ERRCODE_DATATYPE_MISMATCH),
2344					 errmsg("operator class \"%s\" does not accept data type %s",
2345							NameListToString(opcname),
2346							format_type_be(subtype))));
2347	}
2348	else
2349	{
2350		opcid = GetDefaultOpClass(subtype, BTREE_AM_OID);
2351		if (!OidIsValid(opcid))
2352		{
2353			/* We spell the error message identically to ResolveOpClass */
2354			ereport(ERROR,
2355					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2356					 errmsg("data type %s has no default operator class for access method \"%s\"",
2357							format_type_be(subtype), "btree"),
2358					 errhint("You must specify an operator class for the range type or define a default operator class for the subtype.")));
2359		}
2360	}
2361
2362	return opcid;
2363}
2364
2365static Oid
2366findRangeCanonicalFunction(List *procname, Oid typeOid)
2367{
2368	Oid			argList[1];
2369	Oid			procOid;
2370	AclResult	aclresult;
2371
2372	/*
2373	 * Range canonical functions must take and return the range type, and must
2374	 * be immutable.
2375	 */
2376	argList[0] = typeOid;
2377
2378	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 1, argList, true);
2379
2380	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2381		ereport(ERROR,
2382				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2383				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2384						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2385
2386	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != typeOid)
2387		ereport(ERROR,
2388				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2389				 errmsg("range canonical function %s must return range type",
2390						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2391
2392	if (func_volatile(procOid) != PROVOLATILE_IMMUTABLE)
2393		ereport(ERROR,
2394				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2395				 errmsg("range canonical function %s must be immutable",
2396						func_signature_string(procname, 1, NIL, argList))));
2397
2398	/* Also, range type's creator must have permission to call function */
2399	aclresult = object_aclcheck(ProcedureRelationId, procOid, GetUserId(), ACL_EXECUTE);
2400	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
2401		aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_FUNCTION, get_func_name(procOid));
2402
2403	return procOid;
2404}
2405
2406static Oid
2407findRangeSubtypeDiffFunction(List *procname, Oid subtype)
2408{
2409	Oid			argList[2];
2410	Oid			procOid;
2411	AclResult	aclresult;
2412
2413	/*
2414	 * Range subtype diff functions must take two arguments of the subtype,
2415	 * must return float8, and must be immutable.
2416	 */
2417	argList[0] = subtype;
2418	argList[1] = subtype;
2419
2420	procOid = LookupFuncName(procname, 2, argList, true);
2421
2422	if (!OidIsValid(procOid))
2423		ereport(ERROR,
2424				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_FUNCTION),
2425				 errmsg("function %s does not exist",
2426						func_signature_string(procname, 2, NIL, argList))));
2427
2428	if (get_func_rettype(procOid) != FLOAT8OID)
2429		ereport(ERROR,
2430				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2431				 errmsg("range subtype diff function %s must return type %s",
2432						func_signature_string(procname, 2, NIL, argList),
2433						"double precision")));
2434
2435	if (func_volatile(procOid) != PROVOLATILE_IMMUTABLE)
2436		ereport(ERROR,
2437				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
2438				 errmsg("range subtype diff function %s must be immutable",
2439						func_signature_string(procname, 2, NIL, argList))));
2440
2441	/* Also, range type's creator must have permission to call function */
2442	aclresult = object_aclcheck(ProcedureRelationId, procOid, GetUserId(), ACL_EXECUTE);
2443	if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
2444		aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_FUNCTION, get_func_name(procOid));
2445
2446	return procOid;
2447}
2448
2449/*
2450 *	AssignTypeArrayOid
2451 *
2452 *	Pre-assign the type's array OID for use in pg_type.typarray
2453 */
2454Oid
2455AssignTypeArrayOid(void)
2456{
2457	Oid			type_array_oid;
2458
2459	/* Use binary-upgrade override for pg_type.typarray? */
2460	if (IsBinaryUpgrade)
2461	{
2462		if (!OidIsValid(binary_upgrade_next_array_pg_type_oid))
2463			ereport(ERROR,
2464					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
2465					 errmsg("pg_type array OID value not set when in binary upgrade mode")));
2466
2467		type_array_oid = binary_upgrade_next_array_pg_type_oid;
2468		binary_upgrade_next_array_pg_type_oid = InvalidOid;
2469	}
2470	else
2471	{
2472		Relation	pg_type = table_open(TypeRelationId, AccessShareLock);
2473
2474		type_array_oid = GetNewOidWithIndex(pg_type, TypeOidIndexId,
2475											Anum_pg_type_oid);
2476		table_close(pg_type, AccessShareLock);
2477	}
2478
2479	return type_array_oid;
2480}
2481
2482/*
2483 *	AssignTypeMultirangeOid
2484 *
2485 *	Pre-assign the range type's multirange OID for use in pg_type.oid
2486 */
2487Oid
2488AssignTypeMultirangeOid(void)
2489{
2490	Oid			type_multirange_oid;
2491
2492	/* Use binary-upgrade override for pg_type.oid? */
2493	if (IsBinaryUpgrade)
2494	{
2495		if (!OidIsValid(binary_upgrade_next_mrng_pg_type_oid))
2496			ereport(ERROR,
2497					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
2498					 errmsg("pg_type multirange OID value not set when in binary upgrade mode")));
2499
2500		type_multirange_oid = binary_upgrade_next_mrng_pg_type_oid;
2501		binary_upgrade_next_mrng_pg_type_oid = InvalidOid;
2502	}
2503	else
2504	{
2505		Relation	pg_type = table_open(TypeRelationId, AccessShareLock);
2506
2507		type_multirange_oid = GetNewOidWithIndex(pg_type, TypeOidIndexId,
2508												 Anum_pg_type_oid);
2509		table_close(pg_type, AccessShareLock);
2510	}
2511
2512	return type_multirange_oid;
2513}
2514
2515/*
2516 *	AssignTypeMultirangeArrayOid
2517 *
2518 *	Pre-assign the range type's multirange array OID for use in pg_type.typarray
2519 */
2520Oid
2521AssignTypeMultirangeArrayOid(void)
2522{
2523	Oid			type_multirange_array_oid;
2524
2525	/* Use binary-upgrade override for pg_type.oid? */
2526	if (IsBinaryUpgrade)
2527	{
2528		if (!OidIsValid(binary_upgrade_next_mrng_array_pg_type_oid))
2529			ereport(ERROR,
2530					(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
2531					 errmsg("pg_type multirange array OID value not set when in binary upgrade mode")));
2532
2533		type_multirange_array_oid = binary_upgrade_next_mrng_array_pg_type_oid;
2534		binary_upgrade_next_mrng_array_pg_type_oid = InvalidOid;
2535	}
2536	else
2537	{
2538		Relation	pg_type = table_open(TypeRelationId, AccessShareLock);
2539
2540		type_multirange_array_oid = GetNewOidWithIndex(pg_type, TypeOidIndexId,
2541													   Anum_pg_type_oid);
2542		table_close(pg_type, AccessShareLock);
2543	}
2544
2545	return type_multirange_array_oid;
2546}
2547
2548
2549/*-------------------------------------------------------------------
2550 * DefineCompositeType
2551 *
2552 * Create a Composite Type relation.
2553 * `DefineRelation' does all the work, we just provide the correct
2554 * arguments!
2555 *
2556 * If the relation already exists, then 'DefineRelation' will abort
2557 * the xact...
2558 *
2559 * Return type is the new type's object address.
2560 *-------------------------------------------------------------------
2561 */
2562ObjectAddress
2563DefineCompositeType(RangeVar *typevar, List *coldeflist)
2564{
2565	CreateStmt *createStmt = makeNode(CreateStmt);
2566	Oid			old_type_oid;
2567	Oid			typeNamespace;
2568	ObjectAddress address;
2569
2570	/*
2571	 * now set the parameters for keys/inheritance etc. All of these are
2572	 * uninteresting for composite types...
2573	 */
2574	createStmt->relation = typevar;
2575	createStmt->tableElts = coldeflist;
2576	createStmt->inhRelations = NIL;
2577	createStmt->constraints = NIL;
2578	createStmt->options = NIL;
2579	createStmt->oncommit = ONCOMMIT_NOOP;
2580	createStmt->tablespacename = NULL;
2581	createStmt->if_not_exists = false;
2582
2583	/*
2584	 * Check for collision with an existing type name. If there is one and
2585	 * it's an autogenerated array, we can rename it out of the way.  This
2586	 * check is here mainly to get a better error message about a "type"
2587	 * instead of below about a "relation".
2588	 */
2589	typeNamespace = RangeVarGetAndCheckCreationNamespace(createStmt->relation,
2590														 NoLock, NULL);
2591	RangeVarAdjustRelationPersistence(createStmt->relation, typeNamespace);
2592	old_type_oid =
2593		GetSysCacheOid2(TYPENAMENSP, Anum_pg_type_oid,
2594						CStringGetDatum(createStmt->relation->relname),
2595						ObjectIdGetDatum(typeNamespace));
2596	if (OidIsValid(old_type_oid))
2597	{
2598		if (!moveArrayTypeName(old_type_oid, createStmt->relation->relname, typeNamespace))
2599			ereport(ERROR,
2600					(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
2601					 errmsg("type \"%s\" already exists", createStmt->relation->relname)));
2602	}
2603
2604	/*
2605	 * Finally create the relation.  This also creates the type.
2606	 */
2607	DefineRelation(createStmt, RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE, InvalidOid, &address,
2608				   NULL);
2609
2610	return address;
2611}
2612
2613/*
2614 * AlterDomainDefault
2615 *
2616 * Routine implementing ALTER DOMAIN SET/DROP DEFAULT statements.
2617 *
2618 * Returns ObjectAddress of the modified domain.
2619 */
2620ObjectAddress
2621AlterDomainDefault(List *names, Node *defaultRaw)
2622{
2623	TypeName   *typename;
2624	Oid			domainoid;
2625	HeapTuple	tup;
2626	ParseState *pstate;
2627	Relation	rel;
2628	char	   *defaultValue;
2629	Node	   *defaultExpr = NULL; /* NULL if no default specified */
2630	Datum		new_record[Natts_pg_type] = {0};
2631	bool		new_record_nulls[Natts_pg_type] = {0};
2632	bool		new_record_repl[Natts_pg_type] = {0};
2633	HeapTuple	newtuple;
2634	Form_pg_type typTup;
2635	ObjectAddress address;
2636
2637	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
2638	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
2639	domainoid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
2640
2641	/* Look up the domain in the type table */
2642	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2643
2644	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
2645	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2646		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", domainoid);
2647	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2648
2649	/* Check it's a domain and check user has permission for ALTER DOMAIN */
2650	checkDomainOwner(tup);
2651
2652	/* Setup new tuple */
2653
2654	/* Store the new default into the tuple */
2655	if (defaultRaw)
2656	{
2657		/* Create a dummy ParseState for transformExpr */
2658		pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
2659
2660		/*
2661		 * Cook the colDef->raw_expr into an expression. Note: Name is
2662		 * strictly for error message
2663		 */
2664		defaultExpr = cookDefault(pstate, defaultRaw,
2665								  typTup->typbasetype,
2666								  typTup->typtypmod,
2667								  NameStr(typTup->typname),
2668								  0);
2669
2670		/*
2671		 * If the expression is just a NULL constant, we treat the command
2672		 * like ALTER ... DROP DEFAULT.  (But see note for same test in
2673		 * DefineDomain.)
2674		 */
2675		if (defaultExpr == NULL ||
2676			(IsA(defaultExpr, Const) && ((Const *) defaultExpr)->constisnull))
2677		{
2678			/* Default is NULL, drop it */
2679			defaultExpr = NULL;
2680			new_record_nulls[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = true;
2681			new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = true;
2682			new_record_nulls[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = true;
2683			new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = true;
2684		}
2685		else
2686		{
2687			/*
2688			 * Expression must be stored as a nodeToString result, but we also
2689			 * require a valid textual representation (mainly to make life
2690			 * easier for pg_dump).
2691			 */
2692			defaultValue = deparse_expression(defaultExpr,
2693											  NIL, false, false);
2694
2695			/*
2696			 * Form an updated tuple with the new default and write it back.
2697			 */
2698			new_record[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = CStringGetTextDatum(nodeToString(defaultExpr));
2699
2700			new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = true;
2701			new_record[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = CStringGetTextDatum(defaultValue);
2702			new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = true;
2703		}
2704	}
2705	else
2706	{
2707		/* ALTER ... DROP DEFAULT */
2708		new_record_nulls[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = true;
2709		new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefaultbin - 1] = true;
2710		new_record_nulls[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = true;
2711		new_record_repl[Anum_pg_type_typdefault - 1] = true;
2712	}
2713
2714	newtuple = heap_modify_tuple(tup, RelationGetDescr(rel),
2715								 new_record, new_record_nulls,
2716								 new_record_repl);
2717
2718	CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &tup->t_self, newtuple);
2719
2720	/* Rebuild dependencies */
2721	GenerateTypeDependencies(newtuple,
2722							 rel,
2723							 defaultExpr,
2724							 NULL,	/* don't have typacl handy */
2725							 0, /* relation kind is n/a */
2726							 false, /* a domain isn't an implicit array */
2727							 false, /* nor is it any kind of dependent type */
2728							 false, /* don't touch extension membership */
2729							 true); /* We do need to rebuild dependencies */
2730
2731	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(TypeRelationId, domainoid, 0);
2732
2733	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, domainoid);
2734
2735	/* Clean up */
2736	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
2737	heap_freetuple(newtuple);
2738
2739	return address;
2740}
2741
2742/*
2743 * AlterDomainNotNull
2744 *
2745 * Routine implementing ALTER DOMAIN SET/DROP NOT NULL statements.
2746 *
2747 * Returns ObjectAddress of the modified domain.
2748 */
2749ObjectAddress
2750AlterDomainNotNull(List *names, bool notNull)
2751{
2752	TypeName   *typename;
2753	Oid			domainoid;
2754	Relation	typrel;
2755	HeapTuple	tup;
2756	Form_pg_type typTup;
2757	ObjectAddress address = InvalidObjectAddress;
2758
2759	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
2760	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
2761	domainoid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
2762
2763	/* Look up the domain in the type table */
2764	typrel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2765
2766	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
2767	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2768		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", domainoid);
2769	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2770
2771	/* Check it's a domain and check user has permission for ALTER DOMAIN */
2772	checkDomainOwner(tup);
2773
2774	/* Is the domain already set to the desired constraint? */
2775	if (typTup->typnotnull == notNull)
2776	{
2777		table_close(typrel, RowExclusiveLock);
2778		return address;
2779	}
2780
2781	if (notNull)
2782	{
2783		Constraint *constr;
2784
2785		constr = makeNode(Constraint);
2786		constr->contype = CONSTR_NOTNULL;
2787		constr->initially_valid = true;
2788		constr->location = -1;
2789
2790		domainAddNotNullConstraint(domainoid, typTup->typnamespace,
2791								   typTup->typbasetype, typTup->typtypmod,
2792								   constr, NameStr(typTup->typname), NULL);
2793
2794		validateDomainNotNullConstraint(domainoid);
2795	}
2796	else
2797	{
2798		HeapTuple	conTup;
2799		ObjectAddress conobj;
2800
2801		conTup = findDomainNotNullConstraint(domainoid);
2802		if (conTup == NULL)
2803			elog(ERROR, "could not find not-null constraint on domain \"%s\"", NameStr(typTup->typname));
2804
2805		ObjectAddressSet(conobj, ConstraintRelationId, ((Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(conTup))->oid);
2806		performDeletion(&conobj, DROP_RESTRICT, 0);
2807	}
2808
2809	/*
2810	 * Okay to update pg_type row.  We can scribble on typTup because it's a
2811	 * copy.
2812	 */
2813	typTup->typnotnull = notNull;
2814
2815	CatalogTupleUpdate(typrel, &tup->t_self, tup);
2816
2817	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(TypeRelationId, domainoid, 0);
2818
2819	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, domainoid);
2820
2821	/* Clean up */
2822	heap_freetuple(tup);
2823	table_close(typrel, RowExclusiveLock);
2824
2825	return address;
2826}
2827
2828/*
2829 * AlterDomainDropConstraint
2830 *
2831 * Implements the ALTER DOMAIN DROP CONSTRAINT statement
2832 *
2833 * Returns ObjectAddress of the modified domain.
2834 */
2835ObjectAddress
2836AlterDomainDropConstraint(List *names, const char *constrName,
2837						  DropBehavior behavior, bool missing_ok)
2838{
2839	TypeName   *typename;
2840	Oid			domainoid;
2841	HeapTuple	tup;
2842	Relation	rel;
2843	Relation	conrel;
2844	SysScanDesc conscan;
2845	ScanKeyData skey[3];
2846	HeapTuple	contup;
2847	bool		found = false;
2848	ObjectAddress address;
2849
2850	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
2851	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
2852	domainoid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
2853
2854	/* Look up the domain in the type table */
2855	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2856
2857	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
2858	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2859		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", domainoid);
2860
2861	/* Check it's a domain and check user has permission for ALTER DOMAIN */
2862	checkDomainOwner(tup);
2863
2864	/* Grab an appropriate lock on the pg_constraint relation */
2865	conrel = table_open(ConstraintRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2866
2867	/* Find and remove the target constraint */
2868	ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
2869				Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
2870				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2871				ObjectIdGetDatum(InvalidOid));
2872	ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],
2873				Anum_pg_constraint_contypid,
2874				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
2875				ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
2876	ScanKeyInit(&skey[2],
2877				Anum_pg_constraint_conname,
2878				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_NAMEEQ,
2879				CStringGetDatum(constrName));
2880
2881	conscan = systable_beginscan(conrel, ConstraintRelidTypidNameIndexId, true,
2882								 NULL, 3, skey);
2883
2884	/* There can be at most one matching row */
2885	if ((contup = systable_getnext(conscan)) != NULL)
2886	{
2887		Form_pg_constraint construct = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(contup);
2888		ObjectAddress conobj;
2889
2890		if (construct->contype == CONSTRAINT_NOTNULL)
2891		{
2892			((Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup))->typnotnull = false;
2893			CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &tup->t_self, tup);
2894		}
2895
2896		conobj.classId = ConstraintRelationId;
2897		conobj.objectId = construct->oid;
2898		conobj.objectSubId = 0;
2899
2900		performDeletion(&conobj, behavior, 0);
2901		found = true;
2902	}
2903
2904	/* Clean up after the scan */
2905	systable_endscan(conscan);
2906	table_close(conrel, RowExclusiveLock);
2907
2908	if (!found)
2909	{
2910		if (!missing_ok)
2911			ereport(ERROR,
2912					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
2913					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" of domain \"%s\" does not exist",
2914							constrName, TypeNameToString(typename))));
2915		else
2916			ereport(NOTICE,
2917					(errmsg("constraint \"%s\" of domain \"%s\" does not exist, skipping",
2918							constrName, TypeNameToString(typename))));
2919	}
2920
2921	/*
2922	 * We must send out an sinval message for the domain, to ensure that any
2923	 * dependent plans get rebuilt.  Since this command doesn't change the
2924	 * domain's pg_type row, that won't happen automatically; do it manually.
2925	 */
2926	CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(rel, tup, NULL);
2927
2928	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, domainoid);
2929
2930	/* Clean up */
2931	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
2932
2933	return address;
2934}
2935
2936/*
2937 * AlterDomainAddConstraint
2938 *
2939 * Implements the ALTER DOMAIN .. ADD CONSTRAINT statement.
2940 */
2941ObjectAddress
2942AlterDomainAddConstraint(List *names, Node *newConstraint,
2943						 ObjectAddress *constrAddr)
2944{
2945	TypeName   *typename;
2946	Oid			domainoid;
2947	Relation	typrel;
2948	HeapTuple	tup;
2949	Form_pg_type typTup;
2950	Constraint *constr;
2951	char	   *ccbin;
2952	ObjectAddress address = InvalidObjectAddress;
2953
2954	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
2955	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
2956	domainoid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
2957
2958	/* Look up the domain in the type table */
2959	typrel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
2960
2961	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
2962	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
2963		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", domainoid);
2964	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
2965
2966	/* Check it's a domain and check user has permission for ALTER DOMAIN */
2967	checkDomainOwner(tup);
2968
2969	if (!IsA(newConstraint, Constraint))
2970		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
2971			 (int) nodeTag(newConstraint));
2972
2973	constr = (Constraint *) newConstraint;
2974
2975	/* enforced by parser */
2976	Assert(constr->contype == CONSTR_CHECK || constr->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL);
2977
2978	if (constr->contype == CONSTR_CHECK)
2979	{
2980		/*
2981		 * First, process the constraint expression and add an entry to
2982		 * pg_constraint.
2983		 */
2984
2985		ccbin = domainAddCheckConstraint(domainoid, typTup->typnamespace,
2986										 typTup->typbasetype, typTup->typtypmod,
2987										 constr, NameStr(typTup->typname), constrAddr);
2988
2989
2990		/*
2991		 * If requested to validate the constraint, test all values stored in
2992		 * the attributes based on the domain the constraint is being added
2993		 * to.
2994		 */
2995		if (!constr->skip_validation)
2996			validateDomainCheckConstraint(domainoid, ccbin);
2997
2998		/*
2999		 * We must send out an sinval message for the domain, to ensure that
3000		 * any dependent plans get rebuilt.  Since this command doesn't change
3001		 * the domain's pg_type row, that won't happen automatically; do it
3002		 * manually.
3003		 */
3004		CacheInvalidateHeapTuple(typrel, tup, NULL);
3005	}
3006	else if (constr->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL)
3007	{
3008		/* Is the domain already set NOT NULL? */
3009		if (typTup->typnotnull)
3010		{
3011			table_close(typrel, RowExclusiveLock);
3012			return address;
3013		}
3014		domainAddNotNullConstraint(domainoid, typTup->typnamespace,
3015								   typTup->typbasetype, typTup->typtypmod,
3016								   constr, NameStr(typTup->typname), constrAddr);
3017
3018		if (!constr->skip_validation)
3019			validateDomainNotNullConstraint(domainoid);
3020
3021		typTup->typnotnull = true;
3022		CatalogTupleUpdate(typrel, &tup->t_self, tup);
3023	}
3024
3025	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, domainoid);
3026
3027	/* Clean up */
3028	table_close(typrel, RowExclusiveLock);
3029
3030	return address;
3031}
3032
3033/*
3034 * AlterDomainValidateConstraint
3035 *
3036 * Implements the ALTER DOMAIN .. VALIDATE CONSTRAINT statement.
3037 */
3038ObjectAddress
3039AlterDomainValidateConstraint(List *names, const char *constrName)
3040{
3041	TypeName   *typename;
3042	Oid			domainoid;
3043	Relation	typrel;
3044	Relation	conrel;
3045	HeapTuple	tup;
3046	Form_pg_constraint con;
3047	Form_pg_constraint copy_con;
3048	char	   *conbin;
3049	SysScanDesc scan;
3050	Datum		val;
3051	HeapTuple	tuple;
3052	HeapTuple	copyTuple;
3053	ScanKeyData skey[3];
3054	ObjectAddress address;
3055
3056	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
3057	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
3058	domainoid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
3059
3060	/* Look up the domain in the type table */
3061	typrel = table_open(TypeRelationId, AccessShareLock);
3062
3063	tup = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
3064	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
3065		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", domainoid);
3066
3067	/* Check it's a domain and check user has permission for ALTER DOMAIN */
3068	checkDomainOwner(tup);
3069
3070	/*
3071	 * Find and check the target constraint
3072	 */
3073	conrel = table_open(ConstraintRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3074
3075	ScanKeyInit(&skey[0],
3076				Anum_pg_constraint_conrelid,
3077				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3078				ObjectIdGetDatum(InvalidOid));
3079	ScanKeyInit(&skey[1],
3080				Anum_pg_constraint_contypid,
3081				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3082				ObjectIdGetDatum(domainoid));
3083	ScanKeyInit(&skey[2],
3084				Anum_pg_constraint_conname,
3085				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_NAMEEQ,
3086				CStringGetDatum(constrName));
3087
3088	scan = systable_beginscan(conrel, ConstraintRelidTypidNameIndexId, true,
3089							  NULL, 3, skey);
3090
3091	/* There can be at most one matching row */
3092	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tuple = systable_getnext(scan)))
3093		ereport(ERROR,
3094				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3095				 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" of domain \"%s\" does not exist",
3096						constrName, TypeNameToString(typename))));
3097
3098	con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(tuple);
3099	if (con->contype != CONSTRAINT_CHECK)
3100		ereport(ERROR,
3101				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3102				 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" of domain \"%s\" is not a check constraint",
3103						constrName, TypeNameToString(typename))));
3104
3105	val = SysCacheGetAttrNotNull(CONSTROID, tuple, Anum_pg_constraint_conbin);
3106	conbin = TextDatumGetCString(val);
3107
3108	validateDomainCheckConstraint(domainoid, conbin);
3109
3110	/*
3111	 * Now update the catalog, while we have the door open.
3112	 */
3113	copyTuple = heap_copytuple(tuple);
3114	copy_con = (Form_pg_constraint) GETSTRUCT(copyTuple);
3115	copy_con->convalidated = true;
3116	CatalogTupleUpdate(conrel, &copyTuple->t_self, copyTuple);
3117
3118	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(ConstraintRelationId, con->oid, 0);
3119
3120	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, domainoid);
3121
3122	heap_freetuple(copyTuple);
3123
3124	systable_endscan(scan);
3125
3126	table_close(typrel, AccessShareLock);
3127	table_close(conrel, RowExclusiveLock);
3128
3129	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3130
3131	return address;
3132}
3133
3134/*
3135 * Verify that all columns currently using the domain are not null.
3136 */
3137static void
3138validateDomainNotNullConstraint(Oid domainoid)
3139{
3140	List	   *rels;
3141	ListCell   *rt;
3142
3143	/* Fetch relation list with attributes based on this domain */
3144	/* ShareLock is sufficient to prevent concurrent data changes */
3145
3146	rels = get_rels_with_domain(domainoid, ShareLock);
3147
3148	foreach(rt, rels)
3149	{
3150		RelToCheck *rtc = (RelToCheck *) lfirst(rt);
3151		Relation	testrel = rtc->rel;
3152		TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(testrel);
3153		TupleTableSlot *slot;
3154		TableScanDesc scan;
3155		Snapshot	snapshot;
3156
3157		/* Scan all tuples in this relation */
3158		snapshot = RegisterSnapshot(GetLatestSnapshot());
3159		scan = table_beginscan(testrel, snapshot, 0, NULL);
3160		slot = table_slot_create(testrel, NULL);
3161		while (table_scan_getnextslot(scan, ForwardScanDirection, slot))
3162		{
3163			int			i;
3164
3165			/* Test attributes that are of the domain */
3166			for (i = 0; i < rtc->natts; i++)
3167			{
3168				int			attnum = rtc->atts[i];
3169				Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
3170
3171				if (slot_attisnull(slot, attnum))
3172				{
3173					/*
3174					 * In principle the auxiliary information for this error
3175					 * should be errdatatype(), but errtablecol() seems
3176					 * considerably more useful in practice.  Since this code
3177					 * only executes in an ALTER DOMAIN command, the client
3178					 * should already know which domain is in question.
3179					 */
3180					ereport(ERROR,
3181							(errcode(ERRCODE_NOT_NULL_VIOLATION),
3182							 errmsg("column \"%s\" of table \"%s\" contains null values",
3183									NameStr(attr->attname),
3184									RelationGetRelationName(testrel)),
3185							 errtablecol(testrel, attnum)));
3186				}
3187			}
3188		}
3189		ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot);
3190		table_endscan(scan);
3191		UnregisterSnapshot(snapshot);
3192
3193		/* Close each rel after processing, but keep lock */
3194		table_close(testrel, NoLock);
3195	}
3196}
3197
3198/*
3199 * Verify that all columns currently using the domain satisfy the given check
3200 * constraint expression.
3201 */
3202static void
3203validateDomainCheckConstraint(Oid domainoid, const char *ccbin)
3204{
3205	Expr	   *expr = (Expr *) stringToNode(ccbin);
3206	List	   *rels;
3207	ListCell   *rt;
3208	EState	   *estate;
3209	ExprContext *econtext;
3210	ExprState  *exprstate;
3211
3212	/* Need an EState to run ExecEvalExpr */
3213	estate = CreateExecutorState();
3214	econtext = GetPerTupleExprContext(estate);
3215
3216	/* build execution state for expr */
3217	exprstate = ExecPrepareExpr(expr, estate);
3218
3219	/* Fetch relation list with attributes based on this domain */
3220	/* ShareLock is sufficient to prevent concurrent data changes */
3221
3222	rels = get_rels_with_domain(domainoid, ShareLock);
3223
3224	foreach(rt, rels)
3225	{
3226		RelToCheck *rtc = (RelToCheck *) lfirst(rt);
3227		Relation	testrel = rtc->rel;
3228		TupleDesc	tupdesc = RelationGetDescr(testrel);
3229		TupleTableSlot *slot;
3230		TableScanDesc scan;
3231		Snapshot	snapshot;
3232
3233		/* Scan all tuples in this relation */
3234		snapshot = RegisterSnapshot(GetLatestSnapshot());
3235		scan = table_beginscan(testrel, snapshot, 0, NULL);
3236		slot = table_slot_create(testrel, NULL);
3237		while (table_scan_getnextslot(scan, ForwardScanDirection, slot))
3238		{
3239			int			i;
3240
3241			/* Test attributes that are of the domain */
3242			for (i = 0; i < rtc->natts; i++)
3243			{
3244				int			attnum = rtc->atts[i];
3245				Datum		d;
3246				bool		isNull;
3247				Datum		conResult;
3248				Form_pg_attribute attr = TupleDescAttr(tupdesc, attnum - 1);
3249
3250				d = slot_getattr(slot, attnum, &isNull);
3251
3252				econtext->domainValue_datum = d;
3253				econtext->domainValue_isNull = isNull;
3254
3255				conResult = ExecEvalExprSwitchContext(exprstate,
3256													  econtext,
3257													  &isNull);
3258
3259				if (!isNull && !DatumGetBool(conResult))
3260				{
3261					/*
3262					 * In principle the auxiliary information for this error
3263					 * should be errdomainconstraint(), but errtablecol()
3264					 * seems considerably more useful in practice.  Since this
3265					 * code only executes in an ALTER DOMAIN command, the
3266					 * client should already know which domain is in question,
3267					 * and which constraint too.
3268					 */
3269					ereport(ERROR,
3270							(errcode(ERRCODE_CHECK_VIOLATION),
3271							 errmsg("column \"%s\" of table \"%s\" contains values that violate the new constraint",
3272									NameStr(attr->attname),
3273									RelationGetRelationName(testrel)),
3274							 errtablecol(testrel, attnum)));
3275				}
3276			}
3277
3278			ResetExprContext(econtext);
3279		}
3280		ExecDropSingleTupleTableSlot(slot);
3281		table_endscan(scan);
3282		UnregisterSnapshot(snapshot);
3283
3284		/* Hold relation lock till commit (XXX bad for concurrency) */
3285		table_close(testrel, NoLock);
3286	}
3287
3288	FreeExecutorState(estate);
3289}
3290
3291/*
3292 * get_rels_with_domain
3293 *
3294 * Fetch all relations / attributes which are using the domain
3295 *
3296 * The result is a list of RelToCheck structs, one for each distinct
3297 * relation, each containing one or more attribute numbers that are of
3298 * the domain type.  We have opened each rel and acquired the specified lock
3299 * type on it.
3300 *
3301 * We support nested domains by including attributes that are of derived
3302 * domain types.  Current callers do not need to distinguish between attributes
3303 * that are of exactly the given domain and those that are of derived domains.
3304 *
3305 * XXX this is completely broken because there is no way to lock the domain
3306 * to prevent columns from being added or dropped while our command runs.
3307 * We can partially protect against column drops by locking relations as we
3308 * come across them, but there is still a race condition (the window between
3309 * seeing a pg_depend entry and acquiring lock on the relation it references).
3310 * Also, holding locks on all these relations simultaneously creates a non-
3311 * trivial risk of deadlock.  We can minimize but not eliminate the deadlock
3312 * risk by using the weakest suitable lock (ShareLock for most callers).
3313 *
3314 * XXX the API for this is not sufficient to support checking domain values
3315 * that are inside container types, such as composite types, arrays, or
3316 * ranges.  Currently we just error out if a container type containing the
3317 * target domain is stored anywhere.
3318 *
3319 * Generally used for retrieving a list of tests when adding
3320 * new constraints to a domain.
3321 */
3322static List *
3323get_rels_with_domain(Oid domainOid, LOCKMODE lockmode)
3324{
3325	List	   *result = NIL;
3326	char	   *domainTypeName = format_type_be(domainOid);
3327	Relation	depRel;
3328	ScanKeyData key[2];
3329	SysScanDesc depScan;
3330	HeapTuple	depTup;
3331
3332	Assert(lockmode != NoLock);
3333
3334	/* since this function recurses, it could be driven to stack overflow */
3335	check_stack_depth();
3336
3337	/*
3338	 * We scan pg_depend to find those things that depend on the domain. (We
3339	 * assume we can ignore refobjsubid for a domain.)
3340	 */
3341	depRel = table_open(DependRelationId, AccessShareLock);
3342
3343	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
3344				Anum_pg_depend_refclassid,
3345				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3346				ObjectIdGetDatum(TypeRelationId));
3347	ScanKeyInit(&key[1],
3348				Anum_pg_depend_refobjid,
3349				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
3350				ObjectIdGetDatum(domainOid));
3351
3352	depScan = systable_beginscan(depRel, DependReferenceIndexId, true,
3353								 NULL, 2, key);
3354
3355	while (HeapTupleIsValid(depTup = systable_getnext(depScan)))
3356	{
3357		Form_pg_depend pg_depend = (Form_pg_depend) GETSTRUCT(depTup);
3358		RelToCheck *rtc = NULL;
3359		ListCell   *rellist;
3360		Form_pg_attribute pg_att;
3361		int			ptr;
3362
3363		/* Check for directly dependent types */
3364		if (pg_depend->classid == TypeRelationId)
3365		{
3366			if (get_typtype(pg_depend->objid) == TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
3367			{
3368				/*
3369				 * This is a sub-domain, so recursively add dependent columns
3370				 * to the output list.  This is a bit inefficient since we may
3371				 * fail to combine RelToCheck entries when attributes of the
3372				 * same rel have different derived domain types, but it's
3373				 * probably not worth improving.
3374				 */
3375				result = list_concat(result,
3376									 get_rels_with_domain(pg_depend->objid,
3377														  lockmode));
3378			}
3379			else
3380			{
3381				/*
3382				 * Otherwise, it is some container type using the domain, so
3383				 * fail if there are any columns of this type.
3384				 */
3385				find_composite_type_dependencies(pg_depend->objid,
3386												 NULL,
3387												 domainTypeName);
3388			}
3389			continue;
3390		}
3391
3392		/* Else, ignore dependees that aren't user columns of relations */
3393		/* (we assume system columns are never of domain types) */
3394		if (pg_depend->classid != RelationRelationId ||
3395			pg_depend->objsubid <= 0)
3396			continue;
3397
3398		/* See if we already have an entry for this relation */
3399		foreach(rellist, result)
3400		{
3401			RelToCheck *rt = (RelToCheck *) lfirst(rellist);
3402
3403			if (RelationGetRelid(rt->rel) == pg_depend->objid)
3404			{
3405				rtc = rt;
3406				break;
3407			}
3408		}
3409
3410		if (rtc == NULL)
3411		{
3412			/* First attribute found for this relation */
3413			Relation	rel;
3414
3415			/* Acquire requested lock on relation */
3416			rel = relation_open(pg_depend->objid, lockmode);
3417
3418			/*
3419			 * Check to see if rowtype is stored anyplace as a composite-type
3420			 * column; if so we have to fail, for now anyway.
3421			 */
3422			if (OidIsValid(rel->rd_rel->reltype))
3423				find_composite_type_dependencies(rel->rd_rel->reltype,
3424												 NULL,
3425												 domainTypeName);
3426
3427			/*
3428			 * Otherwise, we can ignore relations except those with both
3429			 * storage and user-chosen column types.
3430			 *
3431			 * XXX If an index-only scan could satisfy "col::some_domain" from
3432			 * a suitable expression index, this should also check expression
3433			 * index columns.
3434			 */
3435			if (rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_RELATION &&
3436				rel->rd_rel->relkind != RELKIND_MATVIEW)
3437			{
3438				relation_close(rel, lockmode);
3439				continue;
3440			}
3441
3442			/* Build the RelToCheck entry with enough space for all atts */
3443			rtc = (RelToCheck *) palloc(sizeof(RelToCheck));
3444			rtc->rel = rel;
3445			rtc->natts = 0;
3446			rtc->atts = (int *) palloc(sizeof(int) * RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rel));
3447			result = lappend(result, rtc);
3448		}
3449
3450		/*
3451		 * Confirm column has not been dropped, and is of the expected type.
3452		 * This defends against an ALTER DROP COLUMN occurring just before we
3453		 * acquired lock ... but if the whole table were dropped, we'd still
3454		 * have a problem.
3455		 */
3456		if (pg_depend->objsubid > RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rtc->rel))
3457			continue;
3458		pg_att = TupleDescAttr(rtc->rel->rd_att, pg_depend->objsubid - 1);
3459		if (pg_att->attisdropped || pg_att->atttypid != domainOid)
3460			continue;
3461
3462		/*
3463		 * Okay, add column to result.  We store the columns in column-number
3464		 * order; this is just a hack to improve predictability of regression
3465		 * test output ...
3466		 */
3467		Assert(rtc->natts < RelationGetNumberOfAttributes(rtc->rel));
3468
3469		ptr = rtc->natts++;
3470		while (ptr > 0 && rtc->atts[ptr - 1] > pg_depend->objsubid)
3471		{
3472			rtc->atts[ptr] = rtc->atts[ptr - 1];
3473			ptr--;
3474		}
3475		rtc->atts[ptr] = pg_depend->objsubid;
3476	}
3477
3478	systable_endscan(depScan);
3479
3480	relation_close(depRel, AccessShareLock);
3481
3482	return result;
3483}
3484
3485/*
3486 * checkDomainOwner
3487 *
3488 * Check that the type is actually a domain and that the current user
3489 * has permission to do ALTER DOMAIN on it.  Throw an error if not.
3490 */
3491void
3492checkDomainOwner(HeapTuple tup)
3493{
3494	Form_pg_type typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3495
3496	/* Check that this is actually a domain */
3497	if (typTup->typtype != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
3498		ereport(ERROR,
3499				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3500				 errmsg("%s is not a domain",
3501						format_type_be(typTup->oid))));
3502
3503	/* Permission check: must own type */
3504	if (!object_ownercheck(TypeRelationId, typTup->oid, GetUserId()))
3505		aclcheck_error_type(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, typTup->oid);
3506}
3507
3508/*
3509 * domainAddCheckConstraint - code shared between CREATE and ALTER DOMAIN
3510 */
3511static char *
3512domainAddCheckConstraint(Oid domainOid, Oid domainNamespace, Oid baseTypeOid,
3513						 int typMod, Constraint *constr,
3514						 const char *domainName, ObjectAddress *constrAddr)
3515{
3516	Node	   *expr;
3517	char	   *ccbin;
3518	ParseState *pstate;
3519	CoerceToDomainValue *domVal;
3520	Oid			ccoid;
3521
3522	Assert(constr->contype == CONSTR_CHECK);
3523
3524	/*
3525	 * Assign or validate constraint name
3526	 */
3527	if (constr->conname)
3528	{
3529		if (ConstraintNameIsUsed(CONSTRAINT_DOMAIN,
3530								 domainOid,
3531								 constr->conname))
3532			ereport(ERROR,
3533					(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
3534					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" for domain \"%s\" already exists",
3535							constr->conname, domainName)));
3536	}
3537	else
3538		constr->conname = ChooseConstraintName(domainName,
3539											   NULL,
3540											   "check",
3541											   domainNamespace,
3542											   NIL);
3543
3544	/*
3545	 * Convert the A_EXPR in raw_expr into an EXPR
3546	 */
3547	pstate = make_parsestate(NULL);
3548
3549	/*
3550	 * Set up a CoerceToDomainValue to represent the occurrence of VALUE in
3551	 * the expression.  Note that it will appear to have the type of the base
3552	 * type, not the domain.  This seems correct since within the check
3553	 * expression, we should not assume the input value can be considered a
3554	 * member of the domain.
3555	 */
3556	domVal = makeNode(CoerceToDomainValue);
3557	domVal->typeId = baseTypeOid;
3558	domVal->typeMod = typMod;
3559	domVal->collation = get_typcollation(baseTypeOid);
3560	domVal->location = -1;		/* will be set when/if used */
3561
3562	pstate->p_pre_columnref_hook = replace_domain_constraint_value;
3563	pstate->p_ref_hook_state = domVal;
3564
3565	expr = transformExpr(pstate, constr->raw_expr, EXPR_KIND_DOMAIN_CHECK);
3566
3567	/*
3568	 * Make sure it yields a boolean result.
3569	 */
3570	expr = coerce_to_boolean(pstate, expr, "CHECK");
3571
3572	/*
3573	 * Fix up collation information.
3574	 */
3575	assign_expr_collations(pstate, expr);
3576
3577	/*
3578	 * Domains don't allow variables (this is probably dead code now that
3579	 * add_missing_from is history, but let's be sure).
3580	 */
3581	if (pstate->p_rtable != NIL ||
3582		contain_var_clause(expr))
3583		ereport(ERROR,
3584				(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_COLUMN_REFERENCE),
3585				 errmsg("cannot use table references in domain check constraint")));
3586
3587	/*
3588	 * Convert to string form for storage.
3589	 */
3590	ccbin = nodeToString(expr);
3591
3592	/*
3593	 * Store the constraint in pg_constraint
3594	 */
3595	ccoid =
3596		CreateConstraintEntry(constr->conname,	/* Constraint Name */
3597							  domainNamespace,	/* namespace */
3598							  CONSTRAINT_CHECK, /* Constraint Type */
3599							  false,	/* Is Deferrable */
3600							  false,	/* Is Deferred */
3601							  true, /* Is Enforced */
3602							  !constr->skip_validation, /* Is Validated */
3603							  InvalidOid,	/* no parent constraint */
3604							  InvalidOid,	/* not a relation constraint */
3605							  NULL,
3606							  0,
3607							  0,
3608							  domainOid,	/* domain constraint */
3609							  InvalidOid,	/* no associated index */
3610							  InvalidOid,	/* Foreign key fields */
3611							  NULL,
3612							  NULL,
3613							  NULL,
3614							  NULL,
3615							  0,
3616							  ' ',
3617							  ' ',
3618							  NULL,
3619							  0,
3620							  ' ',
3621							  NULL, /* not an exclusion constraint */
3622							  expr, /* Tree form of check constraint */
3623							  ccbin,	/* Binary form of check constraint */
3624							  true, /* is local */
3625							  0,	/* inhcount */
3626							  false,	/* connoinherit */
3627							  false,	/* conperiod */
3628							  false);	/* is_internal */
3629	if (constrAddr)
3630		ObjectAddressSet(*constrAddr, ConstraintRelationId, ccoid);
3631
3632	/*
3633	 * Return the compiled constraint expression so the calling routine can
3634	 * perform any additional required tests.
3635	 */
3636	return ccbin;
3637}
3638
3639/* Parser pre_columnref_hook for domain CHECK constraint parsing */
3640static Node *
3641replace_domain_constraint_value(ParseState *pstate, ColumnRef *cref)
3642{
3643	/*
3644	 * Check for a reference to "value", and if that's what it is, replace
3645	 * with a CoerceToDomainValue as prepared for us by
3646	 * domainAddCheckConstraint. (We handle VALUE as a name, not a keyword, to
3647	 * avoid breaking a lot of applications that have used VALUE as a column
3648	 * name in the past.)
3649	 */
3650	if (list_length(cref->fields) == 1)
3651	{
3652		Node	   *field1 = (Node *) linitial(cref->fields);
3653		char	   *colname;
3654
3655		colname = strVal(field1);
3656		if (strcmp(colname, "value") == 0)
3657		{
3658			CoerceToDomainValue *domVal = copyObject(pstate->p_ref_hook_state);
3659
3660			/* Propagate location knowledge, if any */
3661			domVal->location = cref->location;
3662			return (Node *) domVal;
3663		}
3664	}
3665	return NULL;
3666}
3667
3668/*
3669 * domainAddNotNullConstraint - code shared between CREATE and ALTER DOMAIN
3670 */
3671static void
3672domainAddNotNullConstraint(Oid domainOid, Oid domainNamespace, Oid baseTypeOid,
3673						   int typMod, Constraint *constr,
3674						   const char *domainName, ObjectAddress *constrAddr)
3675{
3676	Oid			ccoid;
3677
3678	Assert(constr->contype == CONSTR_NOTNULL);
3679
3680	/*
3681	 * Assign or validate constraint name
3682	 */
3683	if (constr->conname)
3684	{
3685		if (ConstraintNameIsUsed(CONSTRAINT_DOMAIN,
3686								 domainOid,
3687								 constr->conname))
3688			ereport(ERROR,
3689					(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
3690					 errmsg("constraint \"%s\" for domain \"%s\" already exists",
3691							constr->conname, domainName)));
3692	}
3693	else
3694		constr->conname = ChooseConstraintName(domainName,
3695											   NULL,
3696											   "not_null",
3697											   domainNamespace,
3698											   NIL);
3699
3700	/*
3701	 * Store the constraint in pg_constraint
3702	 */
3703	ccoid =
3704		CreateConstraintEntry(constr->conname,	/* Constraint Name */
3705							  domainNamespace,	/* namespace */
3706							  CONSTRAINT_NOTNULL,	/* Constraint Type */
3707							  false,	/* Is Deferrable */
3708							  false,	/* Is Deferred */
3709							  true, /* Is Enforced */
3710							  !constr->skip_validation, /* Is Validated */
3711							  InvalidOid,	/* no parent constraint */
3712							  InvalidOid,	/* not a relation constraint */
3713							  NULL,
3714							  0,
3715							  0,
3716							  domainOid,	/* domain constraint */
3717							  InvalidOid,	/* no associated index */
3718							  InvalidOid,	/* Foreign key fields */
3719							  NULL,
3720							  NULL,
3721							  NULL,
3722							  NULL,
3723							  0,
3724							  ' ',
3725							  ' ',
3726							  NULL,
3727							  0,
3728							  ' ',
3729							  NULL, /* not an exclusion constraint */
3730							  NULL,
3731							  NULL,
3732							  true, /* is local */
3733							  0,	/* inhcount */
3734							  false,	/* connoinherit */
3735							  false,	/* conperiod */
3736							  false);	/* is_internal */
3737
3738	if (constrAddr)
3739		ObjectAddressSet(*constrAddr, ConstraintRelationId, ccoid);
3740}
3741
3742
3743/*
3744 * Execute ALTER TYPE RENAME
3745 */
3746ObjectAddress
3747RenameType(RenameStmt *stmt)
3748{
3749	List	   *names = castNode(List, stmt->object);
3750	const char *newTypeName = stmt->newname;
3751	TypeName   *typename;
3752	Oid			typeOid;
3753	Relation	rel;
3754	HeapTuple	tup;
3755	Form_pg_type typTup;
3756	ObjectAddress address;
3757
3758	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
3759	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
3760	typeOid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
3761
3762	/* Look up the type in the type table */
3763	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3764
3765	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
3766	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
3767		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", typeOid);
3768	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3769
3770	/* check permissions on type */
3771	if (!object_ownercheck(TypeRelationId, typeOid, GetUserId()))
3772		aclcheck_error_type(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, typeOid);
3773
3774	/* ALTER DOMAIN used on a non-domain? */
3775	if (stmt->renameType == OBJECT_DOMAIN && typTup->typtype != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
3776		ereport(ERROR,
3777				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3778				 errmsg("%s is not a domain",
3779						format_type_be(typeOid))));
3780
3781	/*
3782	 * If it's a composite type, we need to check that it really is a
3783	 * free-standing composite type, and not a table's rowtype. We want people
3784	 * to use ALTER TABLE not ALTER TYPE for that case.
3785	 */
3786	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE &&
3787		get_rel_relkind(typTup->typrelid) != RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
3788		ereport(ERROR,
3789				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3790				 errmsg("%s is a table's row type",
3791						format_type_be(typeOid)),
3792		/* translator: %s is an SQL ALTER command */
3793				 errhint("Use %s instead.",
3794						 "ALTER TABLE")));
3795
3796	/* don't allow direct alteration of array types, either */
3797	if (IsTrueArrayType(typTup))
3798		ereport(ERROR,
3799				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3800				 errmsg("cannot alter array type %s",
3801						format_type_be(typeOid)),
3802				 errhint("You can alter type %s, which will alter the array type as well.",
3803						 format_type_be(typTup->typelem))));
3804
3805	/* we do allow separate renaming of multirange types, though */
3806
3807	/*
3808	 * If type is composite we need to rename associated pg_class entry too.
3809	 * RenameRelationInternal will call RenameTypeInternal automatically.
3810	 */
3811	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE)
3812		RenameRelationInternal(typTup->typrelid, newTypeName, false, false);
3813	else
3814		RenameTypeInternal(typeOid, newTypeName,
3815						   typTup->typnamespace);
3816
3817	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, typeOid);
3818	/* Clean up */
3819	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
3820
3821	return address;
3822}
3823
3824/*
3825 * Change the owner of a type.
3826 */
3827ObjectAddress
3828AlterTypeOwner(List *names, Oid newOwnerId, ObjectType objecttype)
3829{
3830	TypeName   *typename;
3831	Oid			typeOid;
3832	Relation	rel;
3833	HeapTuple	tup;
3834	HeapTuple	newtup;
3835	Form_pg_type typTup;
3836	AclResult	aclresult;
3837	ObjectAddress address;
3838
3839	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3840
3841	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
3842	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
3843
3844	/* Use LookupTypeName here so that shell types can be processed */
3845	tup = LookupTypeName(NULL, typename, NULL, false);
3846	if (tup == NULL)
3847		ereport(ERROR,
3848				(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
3849				 errmsg("type \"%s\" does not exist",
3850						TypeNameToString(typename))));
3851	typeOid = typeTypeId(tup);
3852
3853	/* Copy the syscache entry so we can scribble on it below */
3854	newtup = heap_copytuple(tup);
3855	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3856	tup = newtup;
3857	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3858
3859	/* Don't allow ALTER DOMAIN on a type */
3860	if (objecttype == OBJECT_DOMAIN && typTup->typtype != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
3861		ereport(ERROR,
3862				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3863				 errmsg("%s is not a domain",
3864						format_type_be(typeOid))));
3865
3866	/*
3867	 * If it's a composite type, we need to check that it really is a
3868	 * free-standing composite type, and not a table's rowtype. We want people
3869	 * to use ALTER TABLE not ALTER TYPE for that case.
3870	 */
3871	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE &&
3872		get_rel_relkind(typTup->typrelid) != RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE)
3873		ereport(ERROR,
3874				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3875				 errmsg("%s is a table's row type",
3876						format_type_be(typeOid)),
3877		/* translator: %s is an SQL ALTER command */
3878				 errhint("Use %s instead.",
3879						 "ALTER TABLE")));
3880
3881	/* don't allow direct alteration of array types, either */
3882	if (IsTrueArrayType(typTup))
3883		ereport(ERROR,
3884				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3885				 errmsg("cannot alter array type %s",
3886						format_type_be(typeOid)),
3887				 errhint("You can alter type %s, which will alter the array type as well.",
3888						 format_type_be(typTup->typelem))));
3889
3890	/* don't allow direct alteration of multirange types, either */
3891	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_MULTIRANGE)
3892	{
3893		Oid			rangetype = get_multirange_range(typeOid);
3894
3895		/* We don't expect get_multirange_range to fail, but cope if so */
3896		ereport(ERROR,
3897				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
3898				 errmsg("cannot alter multirange type %s",
3899						format_type_be(typeOid)),
3900				 OidIsValid(rangetype) ?
3901				 errhint("You can alter type %s, which will alter the multirange type as well.",
3902						 format_type_be(rangetype)) : 0));
3903	}
3904
3905	/*
3906	 * If the new owner is the same as the existing owner, consider the
3907	 * command to have succeeded.  This is for dump restoration purposes.
3908	 */
3909	if (typTup->typowner != newOwnerId)
3910	{
3911		/* Superusers can always do it */
3912		if (!superuser())
3913		{
3914			/* Otherwise, must be owner of the existing object */
3915			if (!object_ownercheck(TypeRelationId, typTup->oid, GetUserId()))
3916				aclcheck_error_type(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, typTup->oid);
3917
3918			/* Must be able to become new owner */
3919			check_can_set_role(GetUserId(), newOwnerId);
3920
3921			/* New owner must have CREATE privilege on namespace */
3922			aclresult = object_aclcheck(NamespaceRelationId, typTup->typnamespace,
3923										newOwnerId,
3924										ACL_CREATE);
3925			if (aclresult != ACLCHECK_OK)
3926				aclcheck_error(aclresult, OBJECT_SCHEMA,
3927							   get_namespace_name(typTup->typnamespace));
3928		}
3929
3930		AlterTypeOwner_oid(typeOid, newOwnerId, true);
3931	}
3932
3933	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, typeOid);
3934
3935	/* Clean up */
3936	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
3937
3938	return address;
3939}
3940
3941/*
3942 * AlterTypeOwner_oid - change type owner unconditionally
3943 *
3944 * This function recurses to handle dependent types (arrays and multiranges).
3945 * It invokes any necessary access object hooks.  If hasDependEntry is true,
3946 * this function modifies the pg_shdepend entry appropriately (this should be
3947 * passed as false only for table rowtypes and dependent types).
3948 *
3949 * This is used by ALTER TABLE/TYPE OWNER commands, as well as by REASSIGN
3950 * OWNED BY.  It assumes the caller has done all needed checks.
3951 */
3952void
3953AlterTypeOwner_oid(Oid typeOid, Oid newOwnerId, bool hasDependEntry)
3954{
3955	Relation	rel;
3956	HeapTuple	tup;
3957	Form_pg_type typTup;
3958
3959	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
3960
3961	tup = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
3962	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
3963		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", typeOid);
3964	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
3965
3966	/*
3967	 * If it's a composite type, invoke ATExecChangeOwner so that we fix up
3968	 * the pg_class entry properly.  That will call back to
3969	 * AlterTypeOwnerInternal to take care of the pg_type entry(s).
3970	 */
3971	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE)
3972		ATExecChangeOwner(typTup->typrelid, newOwnerId, true, AccessExclusiveLock);
3973	else
3974		AlterTypeOwnerInternal(typeOid, newOwnerId);
3975
3976	/* Update owner dependency reference */
3977	if (hasDependEntry)
3978		changeDependencyOnOwner(TypeRelationId, typeOid, newOwnerId);
3979
3980	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(TypeRelationId, typeOid, 0);
3981
3982	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
3983	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
3984}
3985
3986/*
3987 * AlterTypeOwnerInternal - bare-bones type owner change.
3988 *
3989 * This routine simply modifies the owner of a pg_type entry, and recurses
3990 * to handle any dependent types.
3991 */
3992void
3993AlterTypeOwnerInternal(Oid typeOid, Oid newOwnerId)
3994{
3995	Relation	rel;
3996	HeapTuple	tup;
3997	Form_pg_type typTup;
3998	Datum		repl_val[Natts_pg_type];
3999	bool		repl_null[Natts_pg_type];
4000	bool		repl_repl[Natts_pg_type];
4001	Acl		   *newAcl;
4002	Datum		aclDatum;
4003	bool		isNull;
4004
4005	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4006
4007	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
4008	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
4009		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", typeOid);
4010	typTup = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
4011
4012	memset(repl_null, false, sizeof(repl_null));
4013	memset(repl_repl, false, sizeof(repl_repl));
4014
4015	repl_repl[Anum_pg_type_typowner - 1] = true;
4016	repl_val[Anum_pg_type_typowner - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(newOwnerId);
4017
4018	aclDatum = heap_getattr(tup,
4019							Anum_pg_type_typacl,
4020							RelationGetDescr(rel),
4021							&isNull);
4022	/* Null ACLs do not require changes */
4023	if (!isNull)
4024	{
4025		newAcl = aclnewowner(DatumGetAclP(aclDatum),
4026							 typTup->typowner, newOwnerId);
4027		repl_repl[Anum_pg_type_typacl - 1] = true;
4028		repl_val[Anum_pg_type_typacl - 1] = PointerGetDatum(newAcl);
4029	}
4030
4031	tup = heap_modify_tuple(tup, RelationGetDescr(rel), repl_val, repl_null,
4032							repl_repl);
4033
4034	CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &tup->t_self, tup);
4035
4036	/* If it has an array type, update that too */
4037	if (OidIsValid(typTup->typarray))
4038		AlterTypeOwnerInternal(typTup->typarray, newOwnerId);
4039
4040	/* If it is a range type, update the associated multirange too */
4041	if (typTup->typtype == TYPTYPE_RANGE)
4042	{
4043		Oid			multirange_typeid = get_range_multirange(typeOid);
4044
4045		if (!OidIsValid(multirange_typeid))
4046			ereport(ERROR,
4047					(errcode(ERRCODE_UNDEFINED_OBJECT),
4048					 errmsg("could not find multirange type for data type %s",
4049							format_type_be(typeOid))));
4050		AlterTypeOwnerInternal(multirange_typeid, newOwnerId);
4051	}
4052
4053	/* Clean up */
4054	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4055}
4056
4057/*
4058 * Execute ALTER TYPE SET SCHEMA
4059 */
4060ObjectAddress
4061AlterTypeNamespace(List *names, const char *newschema, ObjectType objecttype,
4062				   Oid *oldschema)
4063{
4064	TypeName   *typename;
4065	Oid			typeOid;
4066	Oid			nspOid;
4067	Oid			oldNspOid;
4068	ObjectAddresses *objsMoved;
4069	ObjectAddress myself;
4070
4071	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
4072	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(names);
4073	typeOid = typenameTypeId(NULL, typename);
4074
4075	/* Don't allow ALTER DOMAIN on a non-domain type */
4076	if (objecttype == OBJECT_DOMAIN && get_typtype(typeOid) != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
4077		ereport(ERROR,
4078				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
4079				 errmsg("%s is not a domain",
4080						format_type_be(typeOid))));
4081
4082	/* get schema OID and check its permissions */
4083	nspOid = LookupCreationNamespace(newschema);
4084
4085	objsMoved = new_object_addresses();
4086	oldNspOid = AlterTypeNamespace_oid(typeOid, nspOid, false, objsMoved);
4087	free_object_addresses(objsMoved);
4088
4089	if (oldschema)
4090		*oldschema = oldNspOid;
4091
4092	ObjectAddressSet(myself, TypeRelationId, typeOid);
4093
4094	return myself;
4095}
4096
4097/*
4098 * ALTER TYPE SET SCHEMA, where the caller has already looked up the OIDs
4099 * of the type and the target schema and checked the schema's privileges.
4100 *
4101 * If ignoreDependent is true, we silently ignore dependent types
4102 * (array types and table rowtypes) rather than raising errors.
4103 *
4104 * This entry point is exported for use by AlterObjectNamespace_oid,
4105 * which doesn't want errors when it passes OIDs of dependent types.
4106 *
4107 * Returns the type's old namespace OID, or InvalidOid if we did nothing.
4108 */
4109Oid
4110AlterTypeNamespace_oid(Oid typeOid, Oid nspOid, bool ignoreDependent,
4111					   ObjectAddresses *objsMoved)
4112{
4113	Oid			elemOid;
4114
4115	/* check permissions on type */
4116	if (!object_ownercheck(TypeRelationId, typeOid, GetUserId()))
4117		aclcheck_error_type(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, typeOid);
4118
4119	/* don't allow direct alteration of array types */
4120	elemOid = get_element_type(typeOid);
4121	if (OidIsValid(elemOid) && get_array_type(elemOid) == typeOid)
4122	{
4123		if (ignoreDependent)
4124			return InvalidOid;
4125		ereport(ERROR,
4126				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
4127				 errmsg("cannot alter array type %s",
4128						format_type_be(typeOid)),
4129				 errhint("You can alter type %s, which will alter the array type as well.",
4130						 format_type_be(elemOid))));
4131	}
4132
4133	/* and do the work */
4134	return AlterTypeNamespaceInternal(typeOid, nspOid,
4135									  false,	/* isImplicitArray */
4136									  ignoreDependent,	/* ignoreDependent */
4137									  true, /* errorOnTableType */
4138									  objsMoved);
4139}
4140
4141/*
4142 * Move specified type to new namespace.
4143 *
4144 * Caller must have already checked privileges.
4145 *
4146 * The function automatically recurses to process the type's array type,
4147 * if any.  isImplicitArray should be true only when doing this internal
4148 * recursion (outside callers must never try to move an array type directly).
4149 *
4150 * If ignoreDependent is true, we silently don't process table types.
4151 *
4152 * If errorOnTableType is true, the function errors out if the type is
4153 * a table type.  ALTER TABLE has to be used to move a table to a new
4154 * namespace.  (This flag is ignored if ignoreDependent is true.)
4155 *
4156 * We also do nothing if the type is already listed in *objsMoved.
4157 * After a successful move, we add the type to *objsMoved.
4158 *
4159 * Returns the type's old namespace OID, or InvalidOid if we did nothing.
4160 */
4161Oid
4162AlterTypeNamespaceInternal(Oid typeOid, Oid nspOid,
4163						   bool isImplicitArray,
4164						   bool ignoreDependent,
4165						   bool errorOnTableType,
4166						   ObjectAddresses *objsMoved)
4167{
4168	Relation	rel;
4169	HeapTuple	tup;
4170	Form_pg_type typform;
4171	Oid			oldNspOid;
4172	Oid			arrayOid;
4173	bool		isCompositeType;
4174	ObjectAddress thisobj;
4175
4176	/*
4177	 * Make sure we haven't moved this object previously.
4178	 */
4179	thisobj.classId = TypeRelationId;
4180	thisobj.objectId = typeOid;
4181	thisobj.objectSubId = 0;
4182
4183	if (object_address_present(&thisobj, objsMoved))
4184		return InvalidOid;
4185
4186	rel = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4187
4188	tup = SearchSysCacheCopy1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
4189	if (!HeapTupleIsValid(tup))
4190		elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", typeOid);
4191	typform = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
4192
4193	oldNspOid = typform->typnamespace;
4194	arrayOid = typform->typarray;
4195
4196	/* If the type is already there, we scan skip these next few checks. */
4197	if (oldNspOid != nspOid)
4198	{
4199		/* common checks on switching namespaces */
4200		CheckSetNamespace(oldNspOid, nspOid);
4201
4202		/* check for duplicate name (more friendly than unique-index failure) */
4203		if (SearchSysCacheExists2(TYPENAMENSP,
4204								  NameGetDatum(&typform->typname),
4205								  ObjectIdGetDatum(nspOid)))
4206			ereport(ERROR,
4207					(errcode(ERRCODE_DUPLICATE_OBJECT),
4208					 errmsg("type \"%s\" already exists in schema \"%s\"",
4209							NameStr(typform->typname),
4210							get_namespace_name(nspOid))));
4211	}
4212
4213	/* Detect whether type is a composite type (but not a table rowtype) */
4214	isCompositeType =
4215		(typform->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE &&
4216		 get_rel_relkind(typform->typrelid) == RELKIND_COMPOSITE_TYPE);
4217
4218	/* Enforce not-table-type if requested */
4219	if (typform->typtype == TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE && !isCompositeType)
4220	{
4221		if (ignoreDependent)
4222		{
4223			table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4224			return InvalidOid;
4225		}
4226		if (errorOnTableType)
4227			ereport(ERROR,
4228					(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
4229					 errmsg("%s is a table's row type",
4230							format_type_be(typeOid)),
4231			/* translator: %s is an SQL ALTER command */
4232					 errhint("Use %s instead.", "ALTER TABLE")));
4233	}
4234
4235	if (oldNspOid != nspOid)
4236	{
4237		/* OK, modify the pg_type row */
4238
4239		/* tup is a copy, so we can scribble directly on it */
4240		typform->typnamespace = nspOid;
4241
4242		CatalogTupleUpdate(rel, &tup->t_self, tup);
4243	}
4244
4245	/*
4246	 * Composite types have pg_class entries.
4247	 *
4248	 * We need to modify the pg_class tuple as well to reflect the change of
4249	 * schema.
4250	 */
4251	if (isCompositeType)
4252	{
4253		Relation	classRel;
4254
4255		classRel = table_open(RelationRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4256
4257		AlterRelationNamespaceInternal(classRel, typform->typrelid,
4258									   oldNspOid, nspOid,
4259									   false, objsMoved);
4260
4261		table_close(classRel, RowExclusiveLock);
4262
4263		/*
4264		 * Check for constraints associated with the composite type (we don't
4265		 * currently support this, but probably will someday).
4266		 */
4267		AlterConstraintNamespaces(typform->typrelid, oldNspOid,
4268								  nspOid, false, objsMoved);
4269	}
4270	else
4271	{
4272		/* If it's a domain, it might have constraints */
4273		if (typform->typtype == TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
4274			AlterConstraintNamespaces(typeOid, oldNspOid, nspOid, true,
4275									  objsMoved);
4276	}
4277
4278	/*
4279	 * Update dependency on schema, if any --- a table rowtype has not got
4280	 * one, and neither does an implicit array.
4281	 */
4282	if (oldNspOid != nspOid &&
4283		(isCompositeType || typform->typtype != TYPTYPE_COMPOSITE) &&
4284		!isImplicitArray)
4285		if (changeDependencyFor(TypeRelationId, typeOid,
4286								NamespaceRelationId, oldNspOid, nspOid) != 1)
4287			elog(ERROR, "could not change schema dependency for type \"%s\"",
4288				 format_type_be(typeOid));
4289
4290	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(TypeRelationId, typeOid, 0);
4291
4292	heap_freetuple(tup);
4293
4294	table_close(rel, RowExclusiveLock);
4295
4296	add_exact_object_address(&thisobj, objsMoved);
4297
4298	/* Recursively alter the associated array type, if any */
4299	if (OidIsValid(arrayOid))
4300		AlterTypeNamespaceInternal(arrayOid, nspOid,
4301								   true,	/* isImplicitArray */
4302								   false,	/* ignoreDependent */
4303								   true,	/* errorOnTableType */
4304								   objsMoved);
4305
4306	return oldNspOid;
4307}
4308
4309/*
4310 * AlterType
4311 *		ALTER TYPE <type> SET (option = ...)
4312 *
4313 * NOTE: the set of changes that can be allowed here is constrained by many
4314 * non-obvious implementation restrictions.  Tread carefully when considering
4315 * adding new flexibility.
4316 */
4317ObjectAddress
4318AlterType(AlterTypeStmt *stmt)
4319{
4320	ObjectAddress address;
4321	Relation	catalog;
4322	TypeName   *typename;
4323	HeapTuple	tup;
4324	Oid			typeOid;
4325	Form_pg_type typForm;
4326	bool		requireSuper = false;
4327	AlterTypeRecurseParams atparams;
4328	ListCell   *pl;
4329
4330	catalog = table_open(TypeRelationId, RowExclusiveLock);
4331
4332	/* Make a TypeName so we can use standard type lookup machinery */
4333	typename = makeTypeNameFromNameList(stmt->typeName);
4334	tup = typenameType(NULL, typename, NULL);
4335
4336	typeOid = typeTypeId(tup);
4337	typForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(tup);
4338
4339	/* Process options */
4340	memset(&atparams, 0, sizeof(atparams));
4341	foreach(pl, stmt->options)
4342	{
4343		DefElem    *defel = (DefElem *) lfirst(pl);
4344
4345		if (strcmp(defel->defname, "storage") == 0)
4346		{
4347			char	   *a = defGetString(defel);
4348
4349			if (pg_strcasecmp(a, "plain") == 0)
4350				atparams.storage = TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN;
4351			else if (pg_strcasecmp(a, "external") == 0)
4352				atparams.storage = TYPSTORAGE_EXTERNAL;
4353			else if (pg_strcasecmp(a, "extended") == 0)
4354				atparams.storage = TYPSTORAGE_EXTENDED;
4355			else if (pg_strcasecmp(a, "main") == 0)
4356				atparams.storage = TYPSTORAGE_MAIN;
4357			else
4358				ereport(ERROR,
4359						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_PARAMETER_VALUE),
4360						 errmsg("storage \"%s\" not recognized", a)));
4361
4362			/*
4363			 * Validate the storage request.  If the type isn't varlena, it
4364			 * certainly doesn't support non-PLAIN storage.
4365			 */
4366			if (atparams.storage != TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN && typForm->typlen != -1)
4367				ereport(ERROR,
4368						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4369						 errmsg("fixed-size types must have storage PLAIN")));
4370
4371			/*
4372			 * Switching from PLAIN to non-PLAIN is allowed, but it requires
4373			 * superuser, since we can't validate that the type's C functions
4374			 * will support it.  Switching from non-PLAIN to PLAIN is
4375			 * disallowed outright, because it's not practical to ensure that
4376			 * no tables have toasted values of the type.  Switching among
4377			 * different non-PLAIN settings is OK, since it just constitutes a
4378			 * change in the strategy requested for columns created in the
4379			 * future.
4380			 */
4381			if (atparams.storage != TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN &&
4382				typForm->typstorage == TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN)
4383				requireSuper = true;
4384			else if (atparams.storage == TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN &&
4385					 typForm->typstorage != TYPSTORAGE_PLAIN)
4386				ereport(ERROR,
4387						(errcode(ERRCODE_INVALID_OBJECT_DEFINITION),
4388						 errmsg("cannot change type's storage to PLAIN")));
4389
4390			atparams.updateStorage = true;
4391		}
4392		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "receive") == 0)
4393		{
4394			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4395				atparams.receiveOid =
4396					findTypeReceiveFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel),
4397											typeOid);
4398			else
4399				atparams.receiveOid = InvalidOid;	/* NONE, remove function */
4400			atparams.updateReceive = true;
4401			/* Replacing an I/O function requires superuser. */
4402			requireSuper = true;
4403		}
4404		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "send") == 0)
4405		{
4406			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4407				atparams.sendOid =
4408					findTypeSendFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel),
4409										 typeOid);
4410			else
4411				atparams.sendOid = InvalidOid;	/* NONE, remove function */
4412			atparams.updateSend = true;
4413			/* Replacing an I/O function requires superuser. */
4414			requireSuper = true;
4415		}
4416		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "typmod_in") == 0)
4417		{
4418			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4419				atparams.typmodinOid =
4420					findTypeTypmodinFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel));
4421			else
4422				atparams.typmodinOid = InvalidOid;	/* NONE, remove function */
4423			atparams.updateTypmodin = true;
4424			/* Replacing an I/O function requires superuser. */
4425			requireSuper = true;
4426		}
4427		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "typmod_out") == 0)
4428		{
4429			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4430				atparams.typmodoutOid =
4431					findTypeTypmodoutFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel));
4432			else
4433				atparams.typmodoutOid = InvalidOid; /* NONE, remove function */
4434			atparams.updateTypmodout = true;
4435			/* Replacing an I/O function requires superuser. */
4436			requireSuper = true;
4437		}
4438		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "analyze") == 0)
4439		{
4440			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4441				atparams.analyzeOid =
4442					findTypeAnalyzeFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel),
4443											typeOid);
4444			else
4445				atparams.analyzeOid = InvalidOid;	/* NONE, remove function */
4446			atparams.updateAnalyze = true;
4447			/* Replacing an analyze function requires superuser. */
4448			requireSuper = true;
4449		}
4450		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "subscript") == 0)
4451		{
4452			if (defel->arg != NULL)
4453				atparams.subscriptOid =
4454					findTypeSubscriptingFunction(defGetQualifiedName(defel),
4455												 typeOid);
4456			else
4457				atparams.subscriptOid = InvalidOid; /* NONE, remove function */
4458			atparams.updateSubscript = true;
4459			/* Replacing a subscript function requires superuser. */
4460			requireSuper = true;
4461		}
4462
4463		/*
4464		 * The rest of the options that CREATE accepts cannot be changed.
4465		 * Check for them so that we can give a meaningful error message.
4466		 */
4467		else if (strcmp(defel->defname, "input") == 0 ||
4468				 strcmp(defel->defname, "output") == 0 ||
4469				 strcmp(defel->defname, "internallength") == 0 ||
4470				 strcmp(defel->defname, "passedbyvalue") == 0 ||
4471				 strcmp(defel->defname, "alignment") == 0 ||
4472				 strcmp(defel->defname, "like") == 0 ||
4473				 strcmp(defel->defname, "category") == 0 ||
4474				 strcmp(defel->defname, "preferred") == 0 ||
4475				 strcmp(defel->defname, "default") == 0 ||
4476				 strcmp(defel->defname, "element") == 0 ||
4477				 strcmp(defel->defname, "delimiter") == 0 ||
4478				 strcmp(defel->defname, "collatable") == 0)
4479			ereport(ERROR,
4480					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4481					 errmsg("type attribute \"%s\" cannot be changed",
4482							defel->defname)));
4483		else
4484			ereport(ERROR,
4485					(errcode(ERRCODE_SYNTAX_ERROR),
4486					 errmsg("type attribute \"%s\" not recognized",
4487							defel->defname)));
4488	}
4489
4490	/*
4491	 * Permissions check.  Require superuser if we decided the command
4492	 * requires that, else must own the type.
4493	 */
4494	if (requireSuper)
4495	{
4496		if (!superuser())
4497			ereport(ERROR,
4498					(errcode(ERRCODE_INSUFFICIENT_PRIVILEGE),
4499					 errmsg("must be superuser to alter a type")));
4500	}
4501	else
4502	{
4503		if (!object_ownercheck(TypeRelationId, typeOid, GetUserId()))
4504			aclcheck_error_type(ACLCHECK_NOT_OWNER, typeOid);
4505	}
4506
4507	/*
4508	 * We disallow all forms of ALTER TYPE SET on types that aren't plain base
4509	 * types.  It would for example be highly unsafe, not to mention
4510	 * pointless, to change the send/receive functions for a composite type.
4511	 * Moreover, pg_dump has no support for changing these properties on
4512	 * non-base types.  We might weaken this someday, but not now.
4513	 *
4514	 * Note: if you weaken this enough to allow composite types, be sure to
4515	 * adjust the GenerateTypeDependencies call in AlterTypeRecurse.
4516	 */
4517	if (typForm->typtype != TYPTYPE_BASE)
4518		ereport(ERROR,
4519				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
4520				 errmsg("%s is not a base type",
4521						format_type_be(typeOid))));
4522
4523	/*
4524	 * For the same reasons, don't allow direct alteration of array types.
4525	 */
4526	if (IsTrueArrayType(typForm))
4527		ereport(ERROR,
4528				(errcode(ERRCODE_WRONG_OBJECT_TYPE),
4529				 errmsg("%s is not a base type",
4530						format_type_be(typeOid))));
4531
4532	/* OK, recursively update this type and any arrays/domains over it */
4533	AlterTypeRecurse(typeOid, false, tup, catalog, &atparams);
4534
4535	/* Clean up */
4536	ReleaseSysCache(tup);
4537
4538	table_close(catalog, RowExclusiveLock);
4539
4540	ObjectAddressSet(address, TypeRelationId, typeOid);
4541
4542	return address;
4543}
4544
4545/*
4546 * AlterTypeRecurse: one recursion step for AlterType()
4547 *
4548 * Apply the changes specified by "atparams" to the type identified by
4549 * "typeOid", whose existing pg_type tuple is "tup".  If necessary,
