4550 * recursively update its array type as well.  Then search for any domains
4551 * over this type, and recursively apply (most of) the same changes to those
4552 * domains.
4553 *
4554 * We need this because the system generally assumes that a domain inherits
4555 * many properties from its base type.  See DefineDomain() above for details
4556 * of what is inherited.  Arrays inherit a smaller number of properties,
4557 * but not none.
4558 *
4559 * There's a race condition here, in that some other transaction could
4560 * concurrently add another domain atop this base type; we'd miss updating
4561 * that one.  Hence, be wary of allowing ALTER TYPE to change properties for
4562 * which it'd be really fatal for a domain to be out of sync with its base
4563 * type (typlen, for example).  In practice, races seem unlikely to be an
4564 * issue for plausible use-cases for ALTER TYPE.  If one does happen, it could
4565 * be fixed by re-doing the same ALTER TYPE once all prior transactions have
4566 * committed.
4567 */
4568static void
4569AlterTypeRecurse(Oid typeOid, bool isImplicitArray,
4570				 HeapTuple tup, Relation catalog,
4571				 AlterTypeRecurseParams *atparams)
4572{
4573	Datum		values[Natts_pg_type];
4574	bool		nulls[Natts_pg_type];
4575	bool		replaces[Natts_pg_type];
4576	HeapTuple	newtup;
4577	SysScanDesc scan;
4578	ScanKeyData key[1];
4579	HeapTuple	domainTup;
4580
4581	/* Since this function recurses, it could be driven to stack overflow */
4582	check_stack_depth();
4583
4584	/* Update the current type's tuple */
4585	memset(values, 0, sizeof(values));
4586	memset(nulls, 0, sizeof(nulls));
4587	memset(replaces, 0, sizeof(replaces));
4588
4589	if (atparams->updateStorage)
4590	{
4591		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typstorage - 1] = true;
4592		values[Anum_pg_type_typstorage - 1] = CharGetDatum(atparams->storage);
4593	}
4594	if (atparams->updateReceive)
4595	{
4596		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typreceive - 1] = true;
4597		values[Anum_pg_type_typreceive - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->receiveOid);
4598	}
4599	if (atparams->updateSend)
4600	{
4601		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typsend - 1] = true;
4602		values[Anum_pg_type_typsend - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->sendOid);
4603	}
4604	if (atparams->updateTypmodin)
4605	{
4606		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typmodin - 1] = true;
4607		values[Anum_pg_type_typmodin - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->typmodinOid);
4608	}
4609	if (atparams->updateTypmodout)
4610	{
4611		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typmodout - 1] = true;
4612		values[Anum_pg_type_typmodout - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->typmodoutOid);
4613	}
4614	if (atparams->updateAnalyze)
4615	{
4616		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typanalyze - 1] = true;
4617		values[Anum_pg_type_typanalyze - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->analyzeOid);
4618	}
4619	if (atparams->updateSubscript)
4620	{
4621		replaces[Anum_pg_type_typsubscript - 1] = true;
4622		values[Anum_pg_type_typsubscript - 1] = ObjectIdGetDatum(atparams->subscriptOid);
4623	}
4624
4625	newtup = heap_modify_tuple(tup, RelationGetDescr(catalog),
4626							   values, nulls, replaces);
4627
4628	CatalogTupleUpdate(catalog, &newtup->t_self, newtup);
4629
4630	/* Rebuild dependencies for this type */
4631	GenerateTypeDependencies(newtup,
4632							 catalog,
4633							 NULL,	/* don't have defaultExpr handy */
4634							 NULL,	/* don't have typacl handy */
4635							 0, /* we rejected composite types above */
4636							 isImplicitArray,	/* it might be an array */
4637							 isImplicitArray,	/* dependent iff it's array */
4638							 false, /* don't touch extension membership */
4639							 true);
4640
4641	InvokeObjectPostAlterHook(TypeRelationId, typeOid, 0);
4642
4643	/*
4644	 * Arrays inherit their base type's typmodin and typmodout, but none of
4645	 * the other properties we're concerned with here.  Recurse to the array
4646	 * type if needed.
4647	 */
4648	if (!isImplicitArray &&
4649		(atparams->updateTypmodin || atparams->updateTypmodout))
4650	{
4651		Oid			arrtypoid = ((Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(newtup))->typarray;
4652
4653		if (OidIsValid(arrtypoid))
4654		{
4655			HeapTuple	arrtup;
4656			AlterTypeRecurseParams arrparams;
4657
4658			arrtup = SearchSysCache1(TYPEOID, ObjectIdGetDatum(arrtypoid));
4659			if (!HeapTupleIsValid(arrtup))
4660				elog(ERROR, "cache lookup failed for type %u", arrtypoid);
4661
4662			memset(&arrparams, 0, sizeof(arrparams));
4663			arrparams.updateTypmodin = atparams->updateTypmodin;
4664			arrparams.updateTypmodout = atparams->updateTypmodout;
4665			arrparams.typmodinOid = atparams->typmodinOid;
4666			arrparams.typmodoutOid = atparams->typmodoutOid;
4667
4668			AlterTypeRecurse(arrtypoid, true, arrtup, catalog, &arrparams);
4669
4670			ReleaseSysCache(arrtup);
4671		}
4672	}
4673
4674	/*
4675	 * Now we need to recurse to domains.  However, some properties are not
4676	 * inherited by domains, so clear the update flags for those.
4677	 */
4678	atparams->updateReceive = false;	/* domains use F_DOMAIN_RECV */
4679	atparams->updateTypmodin = false;	/* domains don't have typmods */
4680	atparams->updateTypmodout = false;
4681	atparams->updateSubscript = false;	/* domains don't have subscriptors */
4682
4683	/* Skip the scan if nothing remains to be done */
4684	if (!(atparams->updateStorage ||
4685		  atparams->updateSend ||
4686		  atparams->updateAnalyze))
4687		return;
4688
4689	/* Search pg_type for possible domains over this type */
4690	ScanKeyInit(&key[0],
4691				Anum_pg_type_typbasetype,
4692				BTEqualStrategyNumber, F_OIDEQ,
4693				ObjectIdGetDatum(typeOid));
4694
4695	scan = systable_beginscan(catalog, InvalidOid, false,
4696							  NULL, 1, key);
4697
4698	while ((domainTup = systable_getnext(scan)) != NULL)
4699	{
4700		Form_pg_type domainForm = (Form_pg_type) GETSTRUCT(domainTup);
4701
4702		/*
4703		 * Shouldn't have a nonzero typbasetype in a non-domain, but let's
4704		 * check
4705		 */
4706		if (domainForm->typtype != TYPTYPE_DOMAIN)
4707			continue;
4708
4709		AlterTypeRecurse(domainForm->oid, false, domainTup, catalog, atparams);
4710	}
4711
4712	systable_endscan(scan);
4713}
